prevalence.source: Primary immunodeficiency registry syntheses; Orphanet; Indian immunology center reports
prevalence.trend: stable; increasing diagnosis in tertiary immunology services

burden.dalyPerCapita: very low (rare) but largely preventable with immunoglobulin replacement
burden.mortalityRate: Higher with delayed diagnosis and poor access to IVIG/SCIG

vulnerablePopulations: male infants after maternal Ig wanes
patients without access to immunology and flow cytometry
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Recurrent bacterial infections; low immunoglobulins; absent/very low B cells on flow cytometry; BTK genetic confirmation

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; diagnosis via recurrent infections and immunology work-up
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; best with consistent IVIG/SCIG access and infection prevention

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Clustered in tertiary immunology centers; diagnostic delays common
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: IVIG/SCIG replacement, prompt antibiotics, respiratory physiotherapy if bronchiectasis, vaccination planning (avoid live vaccines in severe antibody deficiency)
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: High out-of-pocket costs, limited immunology specialists, inconsistent immunoglobulin access in some states
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Specialist centers can achieve good outcomes with regular immunoglobulin therapy
